prompt:
Pose-first photorealistic fashion editorial photo: a clearly adult woman in her twenties caught halfway while ducking under a half-open rusty garage shutter. The pose is created by passing through the shutter, not by posing.

Background identity: a colorful rusted urban garage front with a half-open corrugated metal shutter, peeling turquoise and red paint, sticker-bombed panels, faded warning graphics, painted floor arrows, colorful plastic crates, exposed pipes, oil-stained concrete, small neon spill, fictional anime posters, torn event flyers, tape marks, and pop-colored graffiti accents. The scene should feel gritty, colorful, lived-in, and visually loud, not a plain dirty garage.

Pose lock: one hand grips the bottom edge of the half-open shutter at about shoulder height, elbow bent naturally. The other hand stays near the waist, side seam, or thigh to keep the outfit line clean. One foot has already stepped out onto the painted concrete, while the other foot remains just inside the garage threshold. Her torso tilts slightly forward to pass under the shutter, shoulders angled against the hips, waist visible, neck long. The shutter edge creates a strong horizontal line above her body, while the body twists through it. The pose must read as ducking through a garage shutter, not standing in front of it.

Composition lock: vertical 4:5 photorealistic fashion editorial crop, camera around waist-to-chest height with slight low angle. Crop from upper thigh or knee to head if needed. The shutter edge, twisting torso, hand on metal, rusty color blocks, poster wall, and face carry the image.

Gaze lock: direct eye contact as if she noticed the camera while coming out from under the shutter. Chin slightly lowered, expression calm, sharp, and caught mid-motion.

Avoid: anime style, illustration style, plain standing, random crouch, arm-over-head glamour pose, clean garage, real logos, readable text, posters covering the face or torso.
